target.name: Sofar Ocean
target.founder: Tim Janssen, Brad Lassey, Alex Wight

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $75M total raised proxy.

data.narrative:
Sofar Ocean is a Series B-stage company operating in the Ocean & Maritime sector. The company has raised $75M+ to date. The valuation analysis is constrained by the absence of identified public comparables and a limited set of private peer transactions in the frontier ocean-tech space, which reduces the robustness of cross-sectional benchmarking.

The stage-progression method is the primary applicable triangulation framework. Using total capital raised of $75M as a proxy for implied post-money valuation at Series A completion, application of a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B-to-subsequent-stage progression yields an implied valuation of $142.5M. This method assumes the company's capital efficiency and growth trajectory align with median venture outcomes at this stage.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $121M (low) to $164M (high), centered on $142.5M. The range reflects reasonable variance around the stage-progression point estimate but is subject to material uncertainty. The primary limitation is methodological—with only one applicable valuation method and no available peer comp universe, the analysis lacks triangulation across independent approaches. Key uncertainties include the company's current burn rate relative to raised capital, revenue trajectory and unit economics in ocean-sensing applications, and competitive positioning within an emerging sector. A more confident valuation would require either revenue-based metrics or identified private market comparables.
